title: betula pumila subsp. glandulifera
intro: Betula pumila subsp. glandulifera, also known as dwarf birch, is a subspecies of small deciduous tree or shrub that belongs to the Betulaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Betula pumila subsp. glandulifera include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and act as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, can be made by infusing the leaves and twigs in hot water to utilize its medicinal properties.
